title: Giving & Asking Directions
situation: A lost person stops you near a market and asks how to get to the nearest metro station.

phrases.hindi: seedha jaiye, phir left le lena
phrases.english: Go straight, then take a left

phrases.hindi: woh red light ke baad, right side mein hai
phrases.english: It's after the red light, on the right side

phrases.hindi: yahan se paanch minute mein pahunch jaoge
phrases.english: You'll reach in 5 minutes from here

phrases.hindi: uss building ke saamne hai, miss nahi hoga
phrases.english: It's in front of that building, you won't miss it
phrases.context: 'ke saamne' = in front of. 'miss nahi hoga' = can't miss it

phrases.hindi: kisi se bhi pooch lena, sab ko pata hai
phrases.context: Common Indian ending to directions — ask around!

grammar_notes: Hindi uses postpositions (come AFTER the noun): 'ghar mein' = in the house, 'station ke paas' = near the station
'ke' connects nouns to postpositions: 'market ke saamne' = in front of market
'le lena' (take), 'mud jaana' (turn) — compound verbs for directions
culture_notes: Indians give directions using landmarks, not street names: 'gol chakkar ke baad' (after the roundabout)
Everyone will help with directions — sometimes even if they don't know the way
'Thoda aage' (a little ahead) can mean anywhere from 100m to 2km

skill_breakdown.explanation: Unlike English prepositions (in, on, at) that come BEFORE nouns, Hindi postpositions come AFTER. Key ones: mein (in/inside), pe/par (on/at), ke paas (near), ke saamne (in front of), ke peeche (behind), ke upar (above), ke neeche (below).

more_examples.hindi: mere ghar ke paas ek park hai
more_examples.english: there's a park near my house

skill_breakdown.explanation: seedha (straight), left/right (same in Hindi), aage (ahead), peeche (behind), yahan (here), wahan (there), idhar (this side), udhar (that side), andar (inside), bahar (outside).
'Pehle' (first), 'phir' (then), 'uske baad' (after that), 'last mein' (finally). Chain these to give step-by-step instructions. Each step: direction + landmark.

more_examples.hindi: pehle seedha jao, phir signal pe left
more_examples.english: first go straight, then left at the signal

more_examples.hindi: uske baad ek chemist dikhega, wahan se right
more_examples.english: after that you'll see a chemist, turn right from there

practice_prompt: Someone stops you outside Lajpat Nagar market asking for directions to the Lajpat Nagar metro station. You know it's about 10 minutes walking — go straight, cross the main road at the signal, then it's on the left side after a big Haldiram's restaurant. Give them directions using landmarks.
